text: Faraday's first law of electromagnetic induction states:
options: Whenever magnetic flux through a circuit changes, an EMF is induced
Magnetic field is proportional to current
explanation: Faraday's 1st law: changing magnetic flux induces EMF. 2nd law: induced EMF \( = -\frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} \) (Faraday-Neumann law).
name: Faraday's First Law

text: Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction gives the induced EMF as:
explanation: Faraday-Neumann law: \( \varepsilon = -\frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} \). For \(N\) turns: \( \varepsilon = -N\frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} \). Negative sign = Lenz's law.
name: Faraday's Law Formula

text: Lenz's law states that the induced current always:
options: Flows in the same direction as the change
Opposes the cause that produces it (by Faraday's law)
Flows from low to high potential
explanation: Lenz's law: induced current direction is such that its magnetic effect opposes the change in flux causing it. Conservation of energy.
name: Lenz's Law

text: The motional EMF induced in a rod of length \(L\) moving with velocity \(v\) in field \(B\) is:
explanation: Motional EMF: \( \varepsilon = BLv\sin\theta \). Maximum when \(\vec{v}\perp\vec{B}\perp L\): \( \varepsilon = BLv \). Due to Lorentz force on free electrons.
name: Motional EMF

text: The self-inductance \(L\) of a coil is defined by:
explanation: Self-inductance: \( \varepsilon = -L\frac{dI}{dt} \). Also \( L = N\Phi_B/I \). Unit: Henry (H) = Vs/A. Opposes change in current.
name: Self-Inductance Definition

text: The self-inductance of a solenoid with \(N\) turns, length \(l\), area \(A\) is:
explanation: Solenoid self-inductance: \( L = \frac{\mu_0 N^2 A}{l} = \mu_0 n^2 V \), where \(n = N/l\) and \(V = Al\).
name: Self-Inductance of Solenoid

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor

text: Mutual inductance \(M\) between two coils is defined by:
options: \( \varepsilon_2 = M\frac{dI_1}{dt} \) (EMF in coil 2 due to changing current in coil 1)
explanation: Mutual inductance: \( \varepsilon_2 = -M\frac{dI_1}{dt} \). \( M = N_2\Phi_{21}/I_1 \). Unit: Henry. \( M \leq \sqrt{L_1L_2} \) (equality when fully coupled).
name: Mutual Inductance

text: The eddy currents in a conductor placed in a changing magnetic field:
options: Produce heat (energy dissipation) and oppose the change (useful in braking)
explanation: Eddy currents: closed loop currents in bulk conductor in changing \(B\). Cause: heating (loss in motors/transformers). Uses: induction furnace, magnetic braking, speedometers.
name: Eddy Currents

text: The transformer works on the principle of:
explanation: Transformer: mutual induction. \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 = I_1/I_2 \) (ideal). Step-up: \(N_2>N_1\). Step-down: \(N_2<N_1\).
name: Transformer Principle

text: The efficiency of an ideal transformer is:
explanation: Ideal transformer: 100% efficient, \(P_{in} = P_{out}\). Real transformers have losses (copper loss \(I^2R\), iron/core loss, hysteresis, eddy currents).
name: Ideal Transformer Efficiency

text: In the AC generator, the EMF produced is:
explanation: AC generator: \( \varepsilon = NBA\omega\sin(\omega t) = \varepsilon_0\sin(\omega t) \), where \(\varepsilon_0 = NBA\omega\) is peak EMF.
name: AC Generator EMF

text: The magnetic flux \(\Phi_B\) through a loop of area \(A\) in field \(B\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Magnetic flux: \( \Phi_B = \vec{B}\cdot\vec{A} = BA\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{B}\) and area normal \(\hat{n}\). Unit: Weber (Wb).
name: Magnetic Flux Formula

text: The coefficient of coupling between two inductors \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) is:
explanation: Coupling coefficient: \( k = M/\sqrt{L_1L_2} \). \( k=1 \): perfect coupling (all flux links both coils). \( k=0 \): no coupling.
name: Coefficient of Coupling

text: The back-EMF in an electric motor is:
options: In the same direction as applied EMF
explanation: Back-EMF: EMF induced by rotating armature opposing applied EMF (Lenz's law). \( I = (V - \varepsilon_{back})/R \). At full speed, back-EMF approaches applied EMF.
name: Back-EMF in Motor
